exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Control the descent; keep shoulder blades retracted.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Row
exercises.notes: Squeeze the shoulder blades; balances pressing volume.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Stop just shy of lockout to keep tension on the delts.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Drive elbows down and back.

exercises.name: EZ-Bar Biceps Curl
exercises.notes: No swinging; full range.

exercises.name: Triceps Rope Pushdown
exercises.notes: Spread the rope at the bottom.

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.notes: Brace before each rep; depth to comfortable parallel.

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Push hips back; feel the hamstring stretch.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Don't let the lower back round at the bottom.

exercises.name: Seated Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Pause briefly at peak contraction.

exercises.name: Standing Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Full stretch and squeeze each rep.

exercises.name: Hanging Knee Raise
exercises.notes: Curl the pelvis; avoid swinging.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.notes: Slight incline to bias upper chest.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.notes: Lead with the elbows; control the negative.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Lead with the elbows, soft bend.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.notes: Great for shoulder health and posture.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
exercises.notes: Let the arms hang back for a full stretch.

exercises.name: Overhead Triceps Extension
exercises.notes: Keep elbows pointed forward.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Full hip extension; ribs down.

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.notes: Stay tall; control the descent.

exercises.name: Leg Extension
exercises.notes: Pause at the top for a hard quad squeeze.

exercises.name: Lying Leg Curl
exercises.notes: Slow eccentric.

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.notes: Full range; pause at the stretch.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.notes: Round the spine; crunch with the abs, not the hips.

plan.progression: Use double progression: stay at the same load until you hit the top of the rep range on all sets with good form, then add ~2.5-5% load and return to the bottom of the range. Repeat weekly.
plan.general_notes: Warm up with 5-10 minutes of light cardio plus 1-2 ramp-up sets on the first exercise of each session. Aim for 7-9 hours of sleep and adequate protein (~1.6-2.2 g/kg). Deload every 5-6 weeks by cutting volume roughly in half.

exercises.name: Back Squat
exercises.notes: Primary lift; rest 3 minutes between sets.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Keep tension; full lockout.

exercises.name: Chest-Supported Row
exercises.notes: Balances the bench pressing volume.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.notes: Brace hard; neutral spine.

exercises.name: Trap-Bar Deadlift
exercises.notes: Joint-friendly deadlift variation; reset each rep.

exercises.name: Standing Overhead Press
exercises.notes: Squeeze glutes; don't over-arch the lower back.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Full stretch at the top.

exercises.name: Hanging Knee Raise
exercises.notes: Controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Barbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Repeat exposure builds the pressing pattern.

exercises.name: Front Squat
exercises.notes: Lighter than back squat; keep elbows high.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.notes: Squeeze the mid-back.

exercises.name: Face Pull
exercises.notes: Shoulder health accessory.

plan.progression: Linear progression: add a small amount of load (about 2.5 kg lower body, 1-2.5 kg upper body) each week as long as you hit all prescribed reps at the target RPE. When a lift stalls twice, reduce it by 10% and build back up.
plan.general_notes: Warm up thoroughly with ramp-up sets before each main lift. Rest 2-3 minutes on compound lifts so strength, not fatigue, is the limiter. Keep the bar path tight and prioritize technique over adding weight.

exercises.name: Goblet Squat
exercises.notes: Keep the torso tall; controlled tempo.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Bench Press
exercises.notes: Full range; steady pace.

exercises.name: One-Arm Dumbbell Row
exercises.notes: Square the hips; pull to the hip.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
exercises.notes: Hinge at the hips; feel the hamstrings.

exercises.name: Plank
exercises.notes: Brace the whole time.

exercises.name: Reverse Lunge
exercises.notes: Per leg; knee-friendly over walking lunges.

exercises.name: Seated Dumbbell Shoulder Press
exercises.notes: Controlled; no excessive arch.

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.notes: Drive the elbows down.

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.notes: Full hip extension.

exercises.name: Dead Bug
exercises.notes: Keep the lower back flat on the floor.

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.notes: Smooth, controlled reps.

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.notes: Bias the upper chest.

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.notes: Lead with the elbows.

exercises.name: Dumbbell Lateral Raise
exercises.notes: Light weight, strict form.

exercises.name: Cable Crunch
exercises.notes: Crunch with the abs.

plan.progression: Keep rest periods short (45-75 seconds) to keep the heart rate elevated. Progress by adding reps within the range, then load. Pair training with a modest calorie deficit and daily step target for fat loss; let nutrition drive the deficit, not punishing workouts.
plan.general_notes: Warm up with 5 minutes of easy cardio and some dynamic mobility. Strength training preserves muscle in a deficit — keep the loads challenging. Prioritize protein and sleep to support recovery while dieting.
